mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during documented rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, indicating labor market deterioration is decoupled from equity pricing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or the joint signal fails to beat buy-and-hold over consecutive 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns as investors price in improved corporate earnings.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained regimes, yielding negative returns in more than 55% of triggered weeks despite rising production indices.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with subsequent equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 220 threshold, persistent inflation forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of periods following the threshold breach, indicating inflation is consistently priced as transitory rather than structurally persistent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when housing supply constraints decouple construction from actual occupancy, causing false positive signals in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly declines in real gross domestic product confirm an economic contraction, which erodes corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Markets exhibit positive forward returns during confirmed GDP contractions, indicating fiscal stimulus or monetary intervention consistently overrides fundamental economic deterioration.
